title: Mandatory Returns and Unroutable Publication · Execution Trace: Mandatory Return Sequence
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must continuously consume return notifications and correlate them without assuming cross-channel event ordering; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the mandatory return sequence increment in a correlated Go return and route-evidence ledger. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Register and drain return notifications before mandatory publication and correlate content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.

Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Mandatory Return Sequence · Theory Mandatory Return Sequence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 1816 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to keep return, alternate custody, confirmation, and unknown route evidence separate; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Register and drain return notifications before mandatory publication and correlate content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.
